Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search QuickBooks for all open bills and accrual accounts using quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_search_accounts. Find: (a) bill_mosaic_damage_accrual_001 (ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC, $90,000, vendor Heartland Movers, due June 15), (b) bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2 (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, $50,000, vendor U.S. DOT -- PHMSA Hazardous Materials Division, due June 5), (c) BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 (KM-44192-ICR, $1,200, vendor KeyMove Specialty Transport, due April 24), (d) acct_contingent_liability_claims ($90,000 balance), (e) acct_dot_penalty_accrual ($50,000 balance), (f) ACC-6185 Claims & Remediation Expense ($1,200 balance).
- Search emails for the Mosaic damage claim status using search_emails (query: "Mosaic" or "Zara Okoye" or "damage" or "prototype" or similar). Find: (a) Tamara Byrd's formal notification to Emeka about $90K prototype damage (cracked servo motor housing), (b) Emeka's response accepting responsibility and launching an investigation, (c) the incident report. Determine current claim status: vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000, MoveOps direct exposure is $40,000 if Heartland does not accept fault.
- Search emails for DOT compliance status updates using search_emails (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "extension" or "compliance" or similar). Find: (a) original DOT inquiry (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187), (b) extension granted to June 5, (c) Chloe's hazmat audit identifying 4 shipments with 1 documentation gap (Keiko Tanaka placarding cert), (d) Greg Pallone at Swift confirming the placarding was completed at time of transport (signed by driver Mike Becerra at Columbus pickup and countersigned at Seattle delivery), with scanned PDFs of the signed certificate to be sent within 24 hours. Determine whether the $50,000 accrual is still appropriate given the extension and documentation progress.
- Search emails for the KeyMove piano insurance rider using search_emails (query: "KeyMove" or "piano" or "insurance rider" or "Emilia Cruz" or "$1,200" or similar). Find Marcus Thorne's email confirming the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider ties back to Craig Nguyen's April 11 damage report on the Steinway Model B (4.5-5 inch scratch on bass side rim during stairwell extraction). Note: the bill number KM-44192-ICR is found in the QuickBooks bill record, not in Marcus's email.
- Search emails and Slack for the StormCloud goodwill credit error using search_emails (query: "StormCloud" or "goodwill" or "credit" or similar) and conversations_search_messages (search_query: "StormCloud" or "credit" or similar). Find: (a) Mina's original email to Tom Wexler promising $500 goodwill credit for the flight booking error, (b) Marcus Thorne's email and Slack message confirming the credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319 (these document numbers are referenced in emails and Slack, not as standalone QuickBooks records), (c) Julian Brooks corroborating the posting error. Determine that StormCloud never actually received the credit and the books show a liability that does not reflect reality.
- Search Linear for tracking tickets related to the liability items using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "StormCloud" or "Heartland" or similar). Find relevant tickets for: (a) DOT compliance audit issues (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic at state_in_progress, issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert at state_todo, and the 3 DOT doc issues at state_done: issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack), (b) StormCloud account reconstruction issue (linear_issue_a49b4d199139). Note: there is no standalone Linear issue for the Mosaic damage claim -- that information is in CRM engagements and Airtable. Read comments using linear_list_comments to get latest status updates.
- Search Airtable for relocation records related to the liability items using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Relocations). Find: (a) recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta (Mosaic, Completed — record contains detailed damage claim section added by Chloe Vance), (b) hazmat relocation records (Keiko Tanaka, Mei-Lin Zhao, Raj Patel, Joaquin Reyes) to verify DOT compliance status.
- Search CRM for Mosaic account status using crm_search_companies (name: "Mosaic") and crm_list_engagements (company_ids with Mosaic company ID). Find engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 documenting the damage claim, $40K insurance gap, and incident timeline. Note: StormCloud (company_stormcloud) exists in CRM but has no engagement notes -- the StormCloud credit error is documented in emails and Slack, not in CRM engagements.
- Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") to find marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the summary email.
- Send summary email to Marcus Thorne from Alejandro using send_email (sender: alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, recipients: ["marcus.thorne@moveops.com"]). Content should cover each of the 4 liability items with: current status, whether the accrual amount is still appropriate, and a recommendation for each (maintain, adjust, or write off).
- Post key figures in #finance (C005) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C005", payload covering: summary of all open liabilities and accruals, total exposure, any items flagged for adjustment, so Hana has visibility for reconciliation work).
- Add comments to relevant Linear tracking tickets using linear_create_comment on the DOT compliance issues and the StormCloud reconstruction issue, documenting the financial review findings so the teams working those issues know the accrual status.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with a summary of findings and recommendations on the open liabilities.

2 The email to Marcus (visible in the content parameter) addresses the Mosaic $90,000 damage accrual and identifies that MoveOps has approximately $40,000 in direct exposure due to the vendor liability coverage cap of $50,000.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content explicitly discusses the Mosaic $90,000 accrual (ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC), states 'our vendor liability insurance caps at $50,000 per item,' and recommends adjusting to $40,000 as the net exposure. All three key elements are present.
✓Run #2The email content explicitly states the $90,000 accrual, that 'vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item,' and 'MoveOps direct exposure (if Heartland does not accept fault): $40,000.' All key figures are present.
✓Run #3The email content explicitly addresses the Mosaic $90,000 accrual and states that Heartland's vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000/item, leaving MoveOps' maximum direct exposure at $40,000, recommending an adjustment to $40K.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly states the accrual was booked at $90,000, that 'MoveOps vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item,' and recommends reducing 'the accrual to $40,000 — our maximum direct exposure net of the $50,000 vendor liability coverage cap.'
✓Run #5The email content explicitly addresses the Mosaic $90,000 accrual, states the vendor liability coverage cap is $50,000 per item, and identifies a $40,000 coverage gap as MoveOps' direct exposure.
✓Run #6The email content explicitly states 'our vendor liability insurance covers $50,000 per item. The gap — and our actual maximum direct financial exposure — is $40,000' in section 1 (Mosaic). It also references the $90K original accrual and recommends reducing to $40,000.
3 The email to Marcus (visible in the content parameter) addresses the DOT $50,000 penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 or similar reference) and notes that the submission deadline was extended to June 5, 2026.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%

✓Run #2The email content addresses 'DOT/PHMSA PENALTY ACCRUAL — $50,000 (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001)' and explicitly mentions the extension deadline: 'PHMSA granted a 15-calendar-day extension (new deadline: June 5, 2026)'.
✗Run #3The email addresses the DOT $50,000 penalty accrual and references ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, but does not mention a submission deadline extension to June 5, 2026. The email discusses April deadlines for a Linear subtask (April 23) but not a June 5 extension to the PHMSA inquiry.
✓Run #4The email content explicitly references 'ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001' (the DOT penalty accrual), the $50,000 booking, and states 'DOT granted a 15-day extension (new deadline: June 5, 2026).'
✓Run #5The email content references the DOT/PHMSA $50,000 penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) and explicitly states the extension deadline is June 5, 2026.

outcome

Both EST-2026-0287 and INV-2026-0319 are mentioned.
